docs.title: Cisco IOS images for Dynamips
docs.description: GNS3 offers multiple ways to emulate IOS. For older images, we use and maintain Dynamips; an emulator dedicated to emulate some Cisco hardware. Dynamips can run unmodified IOS images. In the new GNS3 1.4, there is a way to run a second category of switches and routers. These are classified as Routing and Switching virtual machines (or R+Svms) for short. What are the differences? Here are the major differences:

docs.title: Non-Native IOU for Windows and OSX
docs.description: IOU is IOS on Unix (Solaris, if you want to be specific). It's a version of IOS compiled to run on workstations. It's faster and lighter than emulation. Cisco transitioned to IOL images, which are x86-64 compatible versions of IOS that natively run in Linux. Since everyone and their kid brother still refers to IOL images as “IOU”, that convention is used for the remainder of this document.

docs.title: Client server SSL encryption
docs.description: GNS3 supports secured network communications between the GNS3 GUI and GNS3 server using SSL. SSL mode is not supported for the GNS3 server on Windows but you can use it for clients. docs.description: Starting with GNS3 2.0,  the NAT node became available. This node allows you to connect a topology to internet via NAT. The Internet node was deprecated in favor of this node, and the Cloud node.

docs.description: Beginning with GNS3 1.4 you have been able to change the symbol for a node (or supply your own). docs.title: Why does GNS3 use UUIDs?
docs.description: A commonly asked  question about GNS3, is why I can’t easily find the files of a node or project names on disk. It’s due to the usage of UUIDs in folder naming.

docs.title: 如何配置和使用 AI 助手
docs.description: GNS3 AI Assistant（GNS3 Copilot）最初作为独立项目开发，详见 github.com/yueguobin/gns3-copilot。该项目由社区开发者 YueGuobin 创建，是一个基于大语言模型的网络自动化助手，支持通过自然语言管理网络设备、诊断故障和自动化配置，已合入 GNS3 3.1 版本。

frontMatter.title: 如何配置和使用 AI 助手

docs.title: 如何使用 AI Skills
docs.description: GNS3 Skills 是一个网络故障排查技能库仓库，用于扩展 AI 助手能力。包含 50 个 YAML 技能文件、815 个故障注入场景，覆盖 60+ 种网络协议和技术（从 PPP 到 SRv6、EVPN），集成到 GNS3 网络仿真环境中，提供自动化故障诊断和学习辅助。

previous.title: 如何配置和使用 AI 助手

next.title: 如何进行故障注入测试

docs.title: 如何进行故障注入测试
docs.description: 故障注入是 GNS3 AI Assistant 的一项功能，用于在网络拓扑中模拟网络故障，帮助用户练习网络故障排查技能。该功能通过 AI 自动分析您的网络拓扑，选择合适的故障类型并注入到项目中。

frontMatter.title: 如何进行故障注入测试

docs.description: GNS3 VM 是运行在虚拟机中的 GNS3 服务器，提供完整的 GNS3 网络仿真环境。它与 GNS3 Web UI 配合使用，提供强大的网络模拟能力。

docs.title: GNS3 3.1 文档
docs.description: 欢迎阅读 GNS3 3.1 版本文档。本文档介绍 GNS3 3.1 的主要功能和使用方法。

docs.title: 3.1 新特性概览
docs.description: GNS3 3.1 版本的核心特性是全面 Web 化，所有操作（控制台、VNC、Wireshark）都可在浏览器中完成，同时引入 AI 辅助和故障注入功能，大幅提升用户体验和网络仿真能力。

previous.title: 如何进行故障注入测试

docs.title: 如何使用设置页面
docs.description: 在项目首页点击右上角的三个点按钮，选择"Settings"进入设置页面。

docs.title: 项目首页
docs.description: GNS3 Web UI 是完全基于浏览器的网络仿真管理界面。登录后首先看到的是项目页面。

previous.title: 如何在 VMware ESXi 中使用 GNS3 VM

docs.title: 如何添加计算节点
docs.description: 本文档描述设置 GNS3 控制器与远程计算节点所需的最低配置。

docs.title: 如何管理镜像
docs.description: 在项目首页点击左上角的 GNS3 Logo，选择"Image Manager"（镜像管理），可以查看和管理所有已上传的镜像文件。

previous.title: 如何配置 Docker 模板

docs.title: 如何管理用户和权限
docs.description: 在项目首页点击左上角的 GNS3 Logo，选择"Management"（用户管理），进入管理页面。

docs.title: 如何监控系统状态
docs.description: 在项目首页点击右上角的三个点按钮，选择"System Status"进入系统状态页面。

docs.title: 新建模板
docs.description: 模板是 GNS3 中定义设备运行方式的配置模板。创建模板后，就可以在项目中添加对应的设备节点。

frontMatter.title: 新建模板
frontMatter.前置知识: 已了解设备镜像的基本概念

next.title: 如何创建 Cisco IOS 模板

docs.title: 项目拓扑
docs.description: Project Map（项目拓扑图）是 GNS3 Web UI 的核心工作区，用于创建、编辑和管理网络拓扑图。这是一个交互式的可视化编辑器，用户可以添加节点、连接设备、配置网络拓扑，并实时控制仿真设备的运行状态。

docs.title: 如何使用 Web 控制台
docs.description: Web Console 是 GNS3 3.1 的新功能，让您直接在浏览器中访问设备的命令行终端，无需安装额外软件或打开多个窗口。

frontMatter.title: 如何使用 Web 控制台

docs.title: 如何使用 Web VNC
docs.description: Web VNC 让您直接在浏览器中访问 VNC 类型的设备（如 QEMU 虚拟机、docker等带有桌面的模拟设备），无需安装 VNC 客户端软件。

previous.title: 如何使用 Web 控制台

docs.title: Web Wireshark
docs.description: Web Wireshark 让您直接在浏览器中分析网络数据包，无需安装 Wireshark 软件。它通过 Docker 容器中的 xpra 技术提供完整的 Wireshark 图形界面，支持实时捕获和深度包检测。

docs.title: How to configure and use AI Assistant
docs.description: GNS3 AI Assistant (GNS3 Copilot) started as a separate project. See github.com/yueguobin/gns3-copilot for details. It was created by community developer YueGuobin. It is a network automation assistant based on large language models. It supports managing network devices, diagnosing faults, and automating configuration using natural language. It has been included in GNS3 3.1.

frontMatter.title: How to configure and use AI Assistant

previous.title: How to manage users and permissions

docs.title: How to use AI Skills
docs.description: GNS3 Skills is a repository of network troubleshooting skills. It is used to expand the AI Assistant's capabilities. It contains 50 YAML skill files and 815 fault injection scenarios. It covers over 60 network protocols and technologies (from PPP to SRv6 and EVPN). It is integrated into the GNS3 network simulation environment. It provides automated fault diagnosis and learning support.

previous.title: How to configure and use AI Assistant

next.title: How to inject faults for testing

docs.title: How to inject faults for testing
docs.description: Fault injection is a feature of the GNS3 AI Assistant. It simulates network problems in your topology. This helps you practice your network troubleshooting skills. The feature uses AI to automatically analyze your network topology, choose the right fault type, and inject it into the project.

frontMatter.title: How to inject faults for testing

docs.title: How to use GNS3 VM
docs.description: GNS3 VM is a GNS3 server that runs inside a virtual machine. It gives you a full GNS3 network simulation environment. It works together with the GNS3 Web UI to provide powerful network simulation.

docs.title: GNS3 3.1 Documentation
docs.description: Welcome to the GNS3 3.1 documentation. This guide explains the main features of GNS3 3.1 and how to use it.

docs.title: 3.1 New Features Overview
docs.description: GNS3 3.1 is all about moving everything to the web. You can now do all operations (console, VNC, Wireshark) directly in your browser. It also adds AI help and fault injection. These changes make the user experience much better and improve network simulation.

previous.title: How to inject faults for testing

docs.title: How to use settings page
docs.description: On the project home page, click the three-dot button at the top right and select "Settings".

next.title: How to manage users and permissions

docs.title: Project Home
docs.description: GNS3 Web UI is a browser-based network simulation management interface. After you log in, the first page you see is the project page.

previous.title: How to use GNS3 VM with VMware ESXi

docs.title: How to add compute nodes
docs.description: This document describes the minimum setup needed for GNS3 controller and remote compute nodes.

docs.title: Image Manager
docs.description: On the project home page, click the GNS3 Logo at the top left and select "Image Manager". Here you can see and manage all uploaded image files.

previous.title: How to configure Docker template preferences

docs.title: How to manage users and permissions
docs.description: On the project home page, click the GNS3 Logo at the top left and select "Management".

frontMatter.title: How to manage users and permissions

next.title: How to configure and use AI Assistant

docs.title: How to monitor system status
docs.description: On the project home page, click the three-dot button at the top right and select "System Status".

docs.title: New Template
docs.description: A template is a configuration that defines how a device runs in GNS3. After you create a template, you can add the device node to your project.

next.title: How to create a Cisco IOS template

docs.title: Project Topology
docs.description: The Project Map is the main work area in the GNS3 Web UI. You use it to create, edit, and manage your network topology. It is an interactive visual editor. You can add nodes, connect devices, set up the network, and control the simulation devices in real time.

docs.title: How to create a Cisco IOS template
docs.description: 📺 Watch the Video Tutorial - Follow along step-by-step to create a Cisco IOS template

docs.title: Template Preferences
docs.description: In the project home page, click the GNS3 Logo at the top left, then select Template Preferences. Here you can see and manage all created templates.

previous.title: How to create a Docker template

next.title: How to configure Built-in template preferences

docs.title: How to configure Built-in template preferences
docs.description: Click the "Built-in" option to enter.

frontMatter.title: How to configure Built-in template preferences

next.title: How to configure Dynamips template preferences

docs.title: How to configure Docker template preferences
docs.description: Docker is a node type in GNS3 based on container technology. Unlike QEMU/Dynamips virtual machines, Docker shares the host machine kernel. It starts very fast.

frontMatter.title: How to configure Docker template preferences

previous.title: How to configure QEMU template preferences

docs.title: How to configure Dynamips template preferences
docs.description: Dynamips is the engine in GNS3 that uses IOS images to simulate Cisco routers. Unlike built-in templates, Dynamips IOS router templates need to be created by the user. You must select the IOS image, choose the platform, configure slots, and so on.

frontMatter.title: How to configure Dynamips template preferences

previous.title: How to configure Built-in template preferences

next.title: How to configure IOU template preferences

docs.title: How to configure IOU template preferences
docs.description: IOU is a native Cisco IOS ELF binary (.iol) that runs directly on Linux. It does not need Dynamips hardware simulation. Its performance is much higher than Dynamips.

frontMatter.title: How to configure IOU template preferences

previous.title: How to configure Dynamips template preferences

next.title: How to configure VPCS template preferences

docs.title: How to configure QEMU template preferences
docs.description: QEMU is the most complex node type in GNS3. It has the most configuration options. It supports full hardware virtualization.

frontMatter.title: How to configure QEMU template preferences

previous.title: How to configure VPCS template preferences

next.title: How to configure Docker template preferences

docs.title: How to configure VPCS template preferences
docs.description: VPCS (Virtual PC Simulator) is a lightweight tool in GNS3 that simulates a PC. Unlike routers or switches, VPCS only simulates a very simple virtual PC environment. It is used to test network connectivity.

frontMatter.title: How to configure VPCS template preferences

previous.title: How to configure IOU template preferences

next.title: How to configure QEMU template preferences

docs.description: 📺 Watch the Video Tutorial - Follow along step-by-step to create a QEMU template

frontMatter.title: How to create a QEMU template

previous.title: How to create a Cisco IOU template

docs.title: How to use Web Console
docs.description: Web Console is a new feature in GNS3 3.1. It lets you access the command-line terminal of your devices directly in the browser. You do not need to install extra software or open many windows.

docs.title: How to use Web VNC
docs.description: Web VNC lets you access VNC-type devices (like QEMU virtual machines, Docker, and other emulated devices with a desktop) directly in your browser. You do not need to install VNC client software.

docs.title: Web Wireshark
docs.description: Web Wireshark lets you analyze network packets directly in your browser. You do not need to install Wireshark software. It uses xpra technology inside a Docker container to give you the full Wireshark graphical interface. It supports real-time capture and deep packet inspection.
